query_strategy_description: 20 web-search queries grouped into 6 buckets that map to the 13-section pricing_strategy structure. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Pricing reports lean harder on primary data than market_analysis — bucket 01 (retail price scraping) is the moat. Avoid named-firm aggregator results; prefer retailer e-commerce sites (Shopee/Tokopedia/Lazada/Tiki for SEA), brand DTC sites, regulator price filings, and credible survey vendors with disclosed methodology.

query_buckets.purpose: Per-SKU current prices + 2-year price history across modern trade / GT / e-commerce / direct -> drives 06_tier_stack + 07_competitor_price_points + 12_promo_norms
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} pack size price tier premium mid mass {{year}}
expected_sources: retailer e-commerce listings (Shopee, Tokopedia, Lazada, Tiki, Watsons, Guardian, Indomaret online flyers)
query_buckets.note: This bucket is the moat. KIRA primary research is the SKU-level price observation — when a query surfaces a live e-commerce listing or flyer, the resulting datum is [primary] (KIRA-collected). When a query surfaces a trade-press table summarizing prices, that's [secondary]. Channel anchor (retailer name + city) is required on every observation.

query_buckets.purpose: Survey-based WTP data by consumer segment -> drives 10_wtp_by_segment + cross-references 05_exec_summary callouts
expected_sources: named survey vendors with disclosed methodology (Nielsen, Kantar, Rakuten Insight, GfK — read but only cite by name if methodology is published)
query_buckets.note: WTP data quality varies wildly. If only a single source surfaces a WTP figure, downgrade to [estimate] with the source as the anchor. Anti-positioning rule still applies — never name-drop the forbidden firms in body copy even if they published the survey.

query_buckets.purpose: Published or inferable price-elasticity coefficients by segment -> drives 09_elasticity
queries: {{industry}} price elasticity emerging market consumer study
expected_sources: central bank working papers on pass-through
query_buckets.note: Elasticity coefficients almost always need KIRA triangulation — single-source [secondary] is rare. Estimate methodology: anchor to a published coefficient for an adjacent category or region, adjust for country income tier and category share-of-wallet. Disclose the anchor explicitly in chart subhead.

query_buckets.purpose: Promotional depth + frequency by channel -> drives 12_promo_norms + 05_exec_summary 'biggest pricing move' callout
query_buckets.note: Like bucket 01, KIRA observes promo data directly from e-commerce + flyer scrapes — that's [primary]. Trade-press summaries of average promo depth are [secondary]. Tier-specific norm: premium goods rarely exceed 15% depth; mass goods rely on 30%+ depth.

query_buckets.purpose: Premium-tier value share, growth, opportunity sizing -> drives 06_tier_stack premium row + 13_premium_case_studies + 05_exec_summary premium-opportunity callout
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} premium segment market size {{year}} share growth
{{country}} luxury aspirational {{industry}} consumer rising middle class
expected_sources: listed-company earnings calls breaking out premium segment
brand press releases on premium launches
query_buckets.note: Premium-tier numbers are often disclosed by listed FMCG players in earnings calls — high-quality [secondary]. Anti-positioning still applies; never name forbidden firms in body copy.

query_buckets.purpose: Subscription / freemium / tiered / dynamic pricing model adoption + outcomes -> drives 11_pricing_models + 13_premium_case_studies (success + failure)
expected_sources: listed-company press releases on pricing changes
regulator filings on tariff/rate changes (telco, insurance)
query_buckets.note: Failure cases are non-negotiable when they exist — actively search for them. Query phrasing 'failed' and 'discontinued' will surface them. If no failure case can be sourced credibly, flag to orchestrator and let 13_premium_case_studies collapse to 1-2 successes only.

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets — bucket 01 results feed leading_player_candidate for buckets 04, 05, 06.
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, Synovate, Ipsos, etc.) — anti-positioning rule. If a survey vendor (Nielsen, Kantar, GfK) is the source AND methodology is disclosed AND they are not on the forbidden list, cite as [secondary]; otherwise downgrade to [estimate] with descriptor 'industry consumer-survey data'.
Prefer e-commerce listings with visible price + timestamp over second-hand price summaries
If a price observation appears in 2+ independent channels within +/-5%, treat as well-anchored [primary] with channel-blended price; flag the channel spread in narrative
If a number appears in only 1 source, either find corroboration or downgrade to [estimate] with anchor commentary

B2B-pricing red flag: if 8+ queries return only 'contact sales' / 'request a quote' / 'enterprise pricing' with no transparent price points, halt the pipeline and route to UC2 — this blueprint is not appropriate for opaque B2B pricing
query_execution_rules.primary_data_collection_protocol: For bucket 01 + 04, when an e-commerce listing or flyer is the source: capture (a) brand, (b) SKU/pack size, (c) current price, (d) timestamp, (e) channel + city anchor. This becomes a [primary] datum. Aggregating 5+ such observations per brand into a category-blended price counts as KIRA primary research.
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} x {{ratio}} = {{output}}. E.g. for elasticity: 'Indonesia FMCG personal-care elasticity -0.8 [secondary academic study] x category-specific adjustment 1.2 (share-of-wallet higher for {{industry}}) -> KIRA estimate -0.96 [estimate]'.
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file structured by query bucket. Top-level keys: price_observations (per-SKU array with channel + timestamp), wtp_by_segment, elasticity_by_segment, promo_norms_by_channel, premium_tier_sizing, pricing_model_cases. Every value tagged with source URL + access date + extraction method.

template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
template_variable_resolution.{{ecommerce_anchor}}: Mapped via lookup: ID->Tokopedia OR Shopee, VN->Shopee OR Tiki, TH->Shopee OR Lazada, PH->Shopee OR Lazada, MY->Shopee OR Lazada, SG->Shopee OR Lazada, KH->Shopee, MM->Shop.com.mm. For categories like beauty add Watsons/Guardian; for grocery add Grab Mart / GoMart / Foodpanda; for fashion add Zalora.
template_variable_resolution.{{leading_player_candidate}}: Filled iteratively after bucket 01 runs — the top 10 SKUs surface 5-8 named brands; later buckets reference these by name.
